svg.title: The workspace drawn as nested boxes, Thinking-in-React style: every box is labeled with the component name React DevTools shows, from AppRoot (the composition root) through ViewModelProvider, ThemeProvider and BootGate to App and its six children. Inside WorkspaceEngine, the two view providers wrap InhouseLayoutEngine, whose SplitNode boxes are drawn to the active tab's layout-tree ratios. The diagram loops through two state changes: maximizing Live Rates (the same components re-render from a new LayoutState, so the boxes glide while the siblings collapse to strips) and clicking the Credit tab (key={activeTab} destroys and remounts the WorkspaceEngine subtree — a hard cut to CREDIT_ROOT's tree: NewRfqPanel as a 330px leaf rail, RfqsPanel over CreditBlotter at 0.62/0.38).
svg.text: The workspace as nested components — every label is the name React DevTools shows
AppRoot composition root — built ONCE per mount (StrictMode-safe useRef): createApp(buildBrowserPorts()) → createViewModel(…)
ViewModelProvider — injects the ViewModel context: every useViewModel() below reads it (the DI seam)
ThemeProvider — reads the theme preference through the ViewModel seam
BootGate — always mounted; the boot splash overlays via BootGatePresenter (§17.4)
App App.tsx — the shell's six children; its only useState: activeTab
AmbientBackground — full-bleed backdrop behind everything below
FxViewProvider — rates/blotter tab, quick filter (consumed by the FX panels)
CreditViewProvider — mounted for every tab; its consumers live in the credit dock
layout engine — one green box per kind:"split" node
panel — one PanelLeaf per amber box, resolved from the registries
resize handle (an <hr> host element — hidden in DevTools)
Every green box renders one kind:"split" node of the active tab's layout tree, drawn to its sizes ratios; every amber box is a kind:"panel" leaf whose
panelId is looked up in appHeadRegistry (title bar) and appPanelRegistry (body) — the only place a panel id ever becomes a React component.
The loop shows two state changes. Maximize dispatches an intent to the LayoutMachine, which reduces a new LayoutState — the SAME components
re-render from new numbers, so the boxes glide (§17.2). A tab click flips App's one useState, and key={activeTab} destroys and remounts the whole
WorkspaceEngine subtree — a hard cut to the new tab's default tree, not a transition (nothing survives, not even your layout tweaks).
